text.title: Project Closure Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Agent marks closure complete based on issue-tracker 'Done' state alone.
antipattern.root-cause: 'Done' is self-reported by the team; acceptance requires a signed artefact from the accepting authority — a different party with independent verification.
antipattern.fix: Block closure until acceptance signature is captured with timestamp and signatory.

antipattern.symptom: Agent generates lessons learned from ticket history and meeting minutes without human contribution.
antipattern.root-cause: Agent-generated lessons are pattern-matched platitudes from training data, not project-specific facts.
antipattern.fix: Require quoted contributions attributed to named participants; reject empty lessons files.

antipattern.symptom: Agent sets final cost to zero because no open invoices appear in the system.
antipattern.root-cause: Net-60 vendor invoices arrive after project close; final cost must be flagged as 'pending invoices' until the invoice window closes.
antipattern.fix: Mark final cost as provisional; close after the contractual invoice window.

antipattern.symptom: Closure packet published without access control; contains salaries, vendor rates, customer data.
antipattern.root-cause: Closure packets contain sensitive data routinely; default publication leaks PII.
antipattern.fix: Apply project-charter access tier to closure packet; redact PII before broad distribution.
